items.question: == vs >= in a requirements file — what's the trade-off?
items.answer: == pins an exact version for maximum reproducibility but blocks updates; >= (a range) allows compatible upgrades and bug fixes but risks pulling a newer version that behaves differently.

items.question: Why might the same requirements.txt produce different installs over time?
items.answer: If dependencies are unpinned or use ranges, newer versions published to PyPI (including transitive deps) get picked up on later installs, causing drift.

items.question: What breaks if you only list direct deps with no pins?
items.answer: A future install can resolve to newer transitive or direct versions with breaking changes, so code that worked before may suddenly fail.
